00020 
00027 #ifndef JELATINE_HEADER_H
00028 #   define JELATINE_HEADER_H (1)
00029 
00030 #include "wrappers.h"
00031 
00032 // Forward declarations
00033 
00034 struct class_t;
00035 
00036 /* Every object allocated on the heap has an header describing it, essentialy
00037  * the header contains information necessary to the garbage-collector to handle
00038  * the object plus meta-data needed by the virtual machine to handle Java
00039  * objects and data used to describe the VM internal structures. Here is the
00040  * layout of an object's description field using the default values:
00041  *
00042  *  0                                                             31
00043  * +------------------------------------------------------------+-+-+
00044  * |                   packed class pointer                     |1|M|
00045  * +------------------------------------------------------------+-+-+
00046  *
00047  * A Java header has bit 30 set to 1 to enable the garbage collector to locate
00048  * it during the sweep phase and bit 31 used as the mark bit. The header used
00049  * by C objects in the Java heap is somewhat different: it has bit 30 always
00050  * set to 0 to denote that this is a C object and mark bit always set:
00051  *
00052  *  0                                                             31
00053  * +------------------------------------------------------------+-+-+
00054  * |                           size                             |0|1|
00055  * +------------------------------------------------------------+-+-+
00056  *
00057  * Note that the header format is the same for 64-bit architectures, the field
00058  * is just larger and bits 62 and 63 are used instead of 30 and 31. */
00059 
00062 typedef uintptr_t header_t;
00063 
00069 #define HEADER_RESERVED (2)
00070 
00071 // Define the shift values and bit masks to handle the object header
00072 
00074 #define HEADER_MARK_SHIFT (0)
00075 
00077 #define HEADER_JAVA_OBJECT_SHIFT (1)
00078 
00080 #define HEADER_PCP_MASK (~(((uintptr_t) 1 << HEADER_RESERVED) - 1))
00081 
00083 #define HEADER_SIZE_MASK HEADER_PCP_MASK
00084 
00085 /******************************************************************************
00086  * Header interface                                                           *
00087  ******************************************************************************/
00088 
00089 #if JEL_POINTER_REVERSAL
00090 extern void header_create_gc_counter(header_t *);
00091 extern void header_restore(header_t *, struct class_t *);
00092 extern uint32_t header_get_count(const header_t *, bool);
00093 extern void header_set_count(header_t *, uint32_t, bool);
00094 extern uint32_t header_get_class_index(const header_t *);
00095 #endif // JEL_POINTER_REVERSAL
00096 
00097 /******************************************************************************
00098  * Header inlined functions                                                   *
00099  ******************************************************************************/
00100 
00105 static inline bool header_is_object(header_t *header)
00106 {
00107     return (*header >> HEADER_JAVA_OBJECT_SHIFT) & 1;
00108 } // header_is_object()
00109 
00115 static inline header_t header_create_object(struct class_t *cl)
00116 {
00117     assert(((uintptr_t) cl & ((1 << HEADER_RESERVED) - 1)) == 0);
00118     return (uintptr_t) cl | (1 << HEADER_JAVA_OBJECT_SHIFT);
00119 } // header_create_object()
00120 
00125 static inline header_t header_create_c(size_t size)
00126 {
00127     assert((size & ((1 << HEADER_RESERVED) - 1)) == 0);
00128     return size | (1 << HEADER_MARK_SHIFT);
00129 } // header_create_c()
00130 
00135 static inline struct class_t *header_get_class(header_t *header)
00136 {
00137     assert(header_is_object(header));
00138     return (struct class_t *) (*((uintptr_t *) header) & HEADER_PCP_MASK);
00139 } // header_get_class()
00140 
00145 static inline size_t header_get_size(header_t *header)
00146 {
00147     assert(!header_is_object(header));
00148     return *header & HEADER_SIZE_MASK;
00149 } // header_get_size()
00150 
00155 static inline bool header_is_marked(header_t *header)
00156 {
00157     return (*header >> HEADER_MARK_SHIFT) & 1;
00158 } // header_is_marked()
00159 
00163 static inline void header_set_mark(header_t *header)
00164 {
00165     *header |= 1 << HEADER_MARK_SHIFT;
00166 } // header_set_mark()
00167 
00171 static inline void header_clear_mark(header_t *header)
00172 {
00173     *header &= ~((uintptr_t) 1 << HEADER_MARK_SHIFT);
00174 } // header_clear_mark()
00175 
00176 #endif // !JELATINE_HEADER_H
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:25 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
